summary: This notebook demonstrates a baseline pipeline for the Child Mind Institute competition, focusing on converting time-series parquet data into descriptive statistics and merging them with tabular demographic and clinical features. It trains a single LightGBM regressor using 5-fold stratified cross-validation, then optimizes rounding thresholds on out-of-fold predictions to maximize the quadratic weighted kappa metric. The author shows how to handle ordinal regression targets by post-processing continuous predictions with learned thresholds rather than relying on native ordinal classifiers. Readers should learn efficient parallel time-series feature extraction, consistent categorical encoding across splits, and threshold optimization for ordinal metrics.
competition_flow: Loads tabular and time-series data, extracts descriptive statistics from the time series, trains a LightGBM regressor with 5-fold stratified CV, optimizes rounding thresholds on out-of-fold predictions, and generates a submission CSV.
data_reading: Reads tabular data via pd.read_csv and time-series data from parquet files in series_train.parquet and series_test.parquet directories.
Uses pd.read_parquet to load each file, drops the 'step' column, and computes .describe() statistics.
data_processing: Merges time-series stats with tabular data on 'id', drops 'id', and selects predefined feature columns.
Fills categorical columns with 'Missing', converts to category dtype, and applies consistent integer mappings derived from the training set to both train and test sets.
features_engineering: Descriptive statistics (mean, std, min, max, quartiles, etc.) extracted from time-series parquet files using .describe().values, creating Stat_0 to Stat_N columns.
No other hand-crafted features are explicitly generated.
ensembling: Averages predictions across 5 CV folds and applies a learned threshold vector (optimized via Nelder-Mead on OOF predictions) to round continuous outputs to ordinal classes for submission.
insights: Parallel processing with ThreadPoolExecutor significantly speeds up loading and processing multiple parquet files.
Ordinal regression targets can be effectively handled by training a regressor and optimizing rounding thresholds on out-of-fold predictions.
Consistent categorical encoding requires building mappings from the training set and applying them identically to the test set to prevent leakage or mismatched indices.
